quote: I was trembling, freaking out more than I ever had… and I stepped on stage the most chill version of myself. That's what it took to win.
tension: Minutes before competing for his sixth title, one of the best-trained memories in the world was shaking in the hallway.
situation: It was his comeback — years of training, going for a sixth championship. And outside the hall, minutes before, Nelson was *trembling*, more rattled than he'd ever been. The preparation was finished. The panic wasn't optional.
principle: He didn't fight the fear or fake calm. He'd been *meditating for years* — so he took five minutes, went somewhere in his head, and let his body settle. Then he walked out the *calmest he'd ever been*.
brain: A skill only shows up if you can quiet the alarm first. The stress response floods the body and *hijacks the attention* your trained skill depends on. Settling your own arousal is how you unlock everything you built.
reframe: Staying calm under pressure isn't luck or temperament. It's a *skill you train* — and it may be the one that decides whether everything else you built actually shows up.
ctaPunch: All the training in the world only counts if you can use it *under pressure*.
